assert(x() == 123)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"

tool_use_result.stdout:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"

content.content:
BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then

371:/// `string.dump(function [, strip])` — serialize a function as binary chunk.
373:/// C: `static int str_dump(lua_State *L)`
374:/// Uses `lua_dump` internally; the writer callback builds a buffer.
375:pub fn str_dump(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
382:    // TODO(port): state.dump_function(strip) needs to produce &[u8].
383:    // In the C code, lua_dump writes to a writer callback that fills a luaL_Buffer.
384:    // In Rust, state.dump() should return Vec<u8> or write to a &mut Vec<u8>.
385:    let bytes = state.dump_function(strip)
386:        .map_err(|_| LuaError::runtime(format_args!("unable to dump given function")))?;
2405:    (b"dump",     str_dump),
2490://                  str_dump requires state.dump_function() which is not yet
2491://                  defined; Phase B wires up the ldump.c port.

delta.text: 991` called `lua_vm::api::dump(...)?` but discarded the `bool` return. `api::dump` returns `Ok(false)` when the
argument isn't a Lua function (e.g. C function `print`); the stub treated that as success and returned an empty `Vec<u8>`. So `string
.dump(print)` succeeded with empty bytes, making the assertion `not pcall(string.dump, print)` at cal

ls.lua:352 fail.

**Fix:** Capture the bool from `api::dump`; when it's `false`, return `L
uaError::runtime("unable to dump given function")` — matching the C `str_dump` behavior where `lua_dump` returns non-zero status

for non-Lua functions.

**New state:** Compile is green. I did not re-run the test per the instructions; the mega-lo
op will re-test. The fix should clear the line-352 assertion; the test may now progress further or pass entirely.

content.text:
Build is green.

**Root cause:** `dump_function` stub at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/state_stub.rs:991` called `lua_vm::api::dump(...)?` but discarded the `bool` return. `api::dump` returns `Ok(false)` when the argument isn't a Lua function (e.g. C function `print`); the stub treated that as success and returned an empty `Vec<u8>`. So `string.dump(print)` succeeded with empty bytes, making the assertion `not pcall(string.dump, print)` at calls.lua:352 fail.

**Fix:** Capture the bool from `api::dump`; when it's `false`, return `LuaError::runtime("unable to dump given function")` — matching the C `str_dump` behavior where `lua_dump` returns non-zero status for non-Lua functions.

**New state:** Compile is green. I did not re-run the test per the instructions; the mega-loop will re-test. The fix should clear the line-352 assertion; the test may now progress further or pass entirely.

result:
Build is green.

**Root cause:** `dump_function` stub at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/state_stub.rs:991` called `lua_vm::api::dump(...)?` but discarded the `bool` return. `api::dump` returns `Ok(false)` when the argument isn't a Lua function (e.g. C function `print`); the stub treated that as success and returned an empty `Vec<u8>`. So `string.dump(print)` succeeded with empty bytes, making the assertion `not pcall(string.dump, print)` at calls.lua:352 fail.

**Fix:** Capture the bool from `api::dump`; when it's `false`, return `LuaError::runtime("unable to dump given function")` — matching the C `str_dump` behavior where `lua_dump` returns non-zero status for non-Lua functions.

**New state:** Compile is green. I did not re-run the test per the instructions; the mega-loop will re-test. The fix should clear the line-352 assertion; the test may now progress further or pass entirely.
